title: Barbeque Fish Kenyan Pilaf
description: A buttery, crisp, midnight-craving kenyan dish featuring a pescatarian-friendly barbeque sauce that simmers with fish and grains for an unforgettable meal.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (176°C). Rinse fish fillets and pat dry.
In a large skillet, melt butter over medium heat. Add onion and garlic; sauté until translucent.
Add tomato paste and cook for another minute. Pour in barbecue sauce and water, stirring well to combine.
Increase heat to high, bring mixture to a boil then reduce heat to low. Place fish fillets in the skillet evenly distributing them on top of the sauce.
Cover with aluminum foil and bake for 20 minutes. Uncover, stir gently and continue baking until all liquid is absorbed (another 10-15 minutes).
While fish bakes, prepare rice according to package instructions. Place cooked rice in a pot with enough water; bring to boil then reduce heat to low.
After 20 minutes of baking the fish, stir-in rice and continue cooking for another 5 minutes or until fully absorbed by sauce.
Remove from oven, let sit for 1 minute before serving.
